reviews.review_description:
The gzme is fine forr a single round or 2, but definitely not one that will hold attention for more than that at one time. Which makes the upgrades and all of that beyond pointless, because as soon as you die or even turn the game off to take a break/ do/ play something else, which you WILL do becausethis is only a quick short session type of gane as is- you lose every single thing... level progress, upgrades, points; everything. Not sure if this is due to an intended pure arcade stye or yet another VR developer trying for the godaweful timewasting Rogue-like mechanics that exist only to waste your time (no permanent progression).
So it's a very throw-away quick play arcade game that, though a cool concept and designed well enough, is overpriced for what it is and only fun for a few rounds with nothing to bring you back.
Nothing saves, zero progression outside of spending well over an hour (if you never die that is) trying to unlock things and see other levels (something hardly anyone at all will do), making this a $10 limited arcade/ mobile game title you might play 2 or 3 times before you stop finding reasons to bother playing again.

It's a good, even great,  foundation, but not for me since there's nothing to keep me coming back to it after only 10+ minutes of play.

developer_response.body:
Hi Developer here, 
Since this review I have added multiplayer, a new upgrade drop system on top of the current shop, new enemies, and a physics rework.  Hope you hop back in a give it a shot :)
